weeks.weekly_summary: Foundation week with 2 easy runs spread across preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Focus on aerobic base development and establishing consistent training rhythm. Recent activity shows ~10 km/week; this block introduces structured polarized training with 2 quality sessions per week starting in Week 2.
weeks.coaching_notes: Athlete has limited recent volume (~10 km/week). Week 1 is intentionally conservative at 20 km to avoid overtraining shock (no jump >50% from baseline). All runs at easy/conversational pace (Zone 2). This week establishes habit and readiness for intensity in Week 2. Cross-training (Football Sat, Gym Fri, Pilates Tue) is respected; no hard runs adjacent. Football is high-intensity; ensure Monday easy run is truly easy (Z2 only) for recovery.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 introduces 2 quality sessions: 1 tempo run and 1 fartlek/intervals. Volume increases to ~28 km. Maintain Monday/Wednesday schedule with cross-training on Tue/Fri/Sat.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or very light walking. Prepare mentally for training block.

days.title: Easy 10km
days.description: Conversational pace, focus on smooth rhythm and nasal breathing. Target pace ~6:30-7:00/km based on recent efforts. No effort — this is aerobic base work.
days.notes: Recovery day before Football on Saturday; keep effort easy for CNS recovery.

days.title: Pilates
days.description: Pilates session. Light intensity; pairs well with easy runs for core stability.
days.notes: Light cross-training; does not impair running recovery.

days.title: Easy 10km
days.description: Second run of the week. Same easy pace as Monday (~6:30-7:00/km). Build aerobic system with consistent low-intensity volume.
days.notes: Maintain conversational pace. This run is 3 days before Gym; ample recovery.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Prepare for Gym day and Football.

days.title: Gym
days.description: Strength and conditioning at gym. Focus on lower-body strength, core, and glute activation to support running.
days.notes: Moderate intensity cross-training. Day before Football; ensure adequate recovery nutrition.

days.title: Football
days.description: High-intensity sport. Serves as anaerobic work for this week (counts toward 20% high-intensity stimulus).
days.notes: Football provides intensity variability. This week's only high-intensity stimulus.

weeks.weekly_summary: Volume increases to 28 km with introduction of 2 quality sessions: 1 tempo run (Monday) and 1 fartlek session (Wednesday). Tempo work builds lactate threshold; fartlek adds aerobic capacity variation. 80% easy (20 km) / 20% hard (8 km) ratio maintained via tempo and fartlek intensities.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 1 response positive; athlete ready for intensity. Tempo run (Monday) is Z3-Z4 threshold work. Fartlek (Wednesday) alternates Z2 and Z4 efforts, improving aerobic flexibility. Football remains Saturday high-intensity stimulus. No hard run the day before/after Football — Monday tempo is 3 days before, Wed fartlek is 3 days before. Recovery days (Tue, Thu, Fri, Sun) allow adaptation. Hydration and sleep critical as volume rises.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 builds to peak training phase. Volume reaches ~34 km. Long run enters picture (recommend Friday or one of Mon/Wed as 'long' effort). Quality sessions shift toward tempo + half-marathon pace work to prepare race fitness.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest and recovery.

days.title: Tempo Run: 2km easy + 6km @ HMP + 1km easy
days.description: Warm up 2km easy (Z2, ~6:45/km). Main set: 6km at Half-Marathon pace (~6:00/km, comfortably hard Z3-Z4). Cool down 1km easy. Focus on steady effort and controlled breathing during main set. This is lactate threshold work.
days.key_focus: Lactate threshold development, half-marathon pace familiarization
days.notes: 3 days before Football. Ensure full recovery Mon→Tue→Sat. Heart rate should settle to Z3 upper / Z4 lower during main set.

days.title: Pilates
days.description: Light pilates. Recovery day; no high intensity.
days.notes: Recovery-focused day after tempo run.

days.title: Fartlek: 2km easy + 8x (2min hard / 2min easy) + 1km easy
days.description: Warm up 2km easy (Z2). Main set: 8 repeats of 2min hard (Z4, ~5:30/km effort) / 2min easy recovery (Z2, ~6:45/km). Cool down 1km easy. Fartlek builds aerobic capacity and anaerobic tolerance. Total hard time = 16min.
days.notes: 3 days before Football. Keep easy intervals truly easy for recovery. Hard repeats should feel challenging but sustainable (can speak 1-2 words).

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest.

days.title: Gym
days.description: Strength and conditioning. Lower body and core focus.

days.title: Football
days.description: High-intensity sport. Second week of maintaining Football stimulus.
days.notes: This provides additional intensity stimulus beyond running sessions.

weeks.weekly_summary: Peak training block. Volume increases to 34 km with 2 quality sessions: 1 long run with marathon-pace segments (Monday, 11km total) and 1 tempo + VO2 workout (Wednesday). This week builds race-specific fitness. Long run introduces sustained HMP work; tempo run focuses on threshold maintenance.
weeks.coaching_notes: Athlete is now 12 weeks from race. Week 3 is peak stimulus week before taper begins Week 4. Long run (Monday) reaches 11km with 6km at HMP embedded — this is the key half-marathon specific workout. Wed tempo (9km total) maintains lactate threshold. Football continues Sat as anaerobic stimulus. Monitor fatigue closely: if HRV drops significantly or leg heaviness persists >2 days, dial back Wed workout. Total volume is 34km; this represents 4-week peak of ~240% baseline, which is aggressive but appropriate for 15-week total plan.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 is taper week. Volume drops to 22 km (~35% reduction from Week 3). All runs easy/conversational; replace tempo/fartlek with light 30-min runs. This allows CNS and muscular recovery before race week. Football skipped Week 4 to avoid unnecessary fatigue; only light Pilates and Gym maintained for mobility.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest.

days.title: Long Run: 2km easy + 6km @ HMP + 3km easy
days.description: Warm up 2km easy (Z2, ~6:45/km). Main set: 6km at Half-Marathon Pace (~6:00/km, comfortable but controlled Z3). Cool down 3km easy (Z2). This is the peak long run, introducing sustained HMP work — critical for race readiness. Focus on running smoothly and efficiently at race pace.
days.notes: 3 days before Football. This is the most important run of the block — dial in race pace and mental toughness. Stay well-hydrated.

days.title: Pilates
days.description: Light pilates for recovery and mobility.
days.notes: Recovery day after long run.

days.title: Tempo + VO2: 2km easy + 4km @ tempo + 3x(3min VO2) + 1km easy
days.description: Warm up 2km easy (Z2). Tempo set: 4km at lactate threshold (~6:15/km, Z3-Z4, 'comfortably hard'). VO2 set: 3 x 3min hard (Z4-5, ~5:45/km, hard breathing) with 2min easy recovery between. Cool down 1km easy. This complex workout maintains threshold while building VO2 max.
days.key_focus: Lactate threshold + VO2 max integration, speed reserve
days.notes: 3 days before Football. Complex session; ensure adequate recovery nutrition and sleep. Hard intervals should feel challenging but controlled.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest.

days.title: Gym
days.description: Strength and conditioning. Maintain lower-body and core strength.

days.title: Football
days.description: High-intensity sport. Final high-intensity Football session before taper.
days.notes: After this week, Football is reduced in taper to prevent unnecessary fatigue.

weeks.weekly_summary: Taper week: volume reduced 35% to 22 km. All runs are easy/conversational (Z2 only). No tempo, fartlek, or VO2 work — intensity replaced with strides (short, fast, non-fatiguing). Cross-training continues light (Pilates, Gym) for mobility and strength maintenance, but Football is skipped to preserve energy. This week prioritizes recovery, nervous system repair, and mental preparation for race.
weeks.coaching_notes: Taper begins this week, 11 days before race. Volume drops 35% from Week 3 (34 km → 22 km). Intensity removed; all runs easy (Z2 conversational pace). Two 6km easy runs (Mon, Wed) + strides twice per week (added on days with easy runs for CNS activation without fatigue). No cross-training Football this week. Light Pilates and Gym continue for flexibility and injury prevention. Athlete may feel 'sluggish' or anxious in taper — this is normal. Trust the training; peak fitness arrives race day. Nutrition, sleep, and hydration are critical. Expect modest fitness gains from rest and recovery.
weeks.next_week_preview: Race week (Week 5, not in this plan) begins 7 days after Week 4 ends. Race is scheduled 11 days from end of Week 3. Athlete should rest almost entirely Week 5 until race day with 1-2 very easy 2km shakeout runs mid-week. Target race pace: 6:00/km for 21.1km = 2:06:06. Coach recommends aiming for splits: 10km @ 6:02/km, then 11.1km @ 5:58/km (negative split).

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Mental recovery and reflection on training block.

days.title: Easy 6km + 6x100m strides
days.description: 6km at easy conversational pace (Z2, ~6:45/km). After 10min rest, perform 6 x 100m strides: 20sec hard accelerations with 40sec easy jog recovery between. Strides are short (100m = ~20sec effort), fast (Z4 effort), non-fatiguing, and prime the nervous system for race-like effort without inducing fatigue. Total distance 6.5km including strides.
days.key_focus: CNS activation, maintaining leg speed, easy aerobic work
days.notes: Taper protocol: easy running with strides maintains turnover and power without heavy training load. This is a standard taper run.

days.title: Pilates
days.description: Light pilates. Mobility and core maintenance in taper.
days.notes: Keep intensity light; focus on stretching and mobility.

days.title: Easy 6km + 6x100m strides
days.description: Identical to Monday's session: 6km easy (Z2, ~6:45/km) + 6 x 100m strides (Z4, 20sec hard / 40sec recovery). Maintain form and cadence during strides.
days.notes: Mirror workout to Monday. Consistency in taper is key; don't overshoot effort.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest.

days.title: Gym
days.description: Very light gym session. Focus on mobility, stretching, and light strength. No heavy lifting. Goal is flexibility and mental activation, not strength gain.
days.notes: Reduce gym intensity from 50min Mod to 40min Light during taper.

days.title: Rest Day (Race Prep)
days.description: Complete rest. No Football. Final preparation day before race week. Focus on hydration, nutrition, sleep, and mental readiness.
days.notes: Football skipped in taper. Use day for race logistics, bib pickup, course review, gear prep.

coaching_overview:
4-Week Half-Marathon Training Block (Base → Peak → Taper)

**Block Structure:**
- Week 1 (Base): 20 km, 100% easy. Conservative entry due to low baseline volume (~10 km/week). All Z2 conversational runs.
- Week 2 (Aerobic Dev): 28 km, 80% easy / 20% hard. Introduces 2 quality sessions: tempo run (6km @ HMP) and fartlek (8x 2min hard). Football provides weekend anaerobic stimulus.
- Week 3 (Peak): 34 km, 78% easy / 22% hard. Peak training stimulus. Long run reaches 11km with 6km @ HMP (race-specific). Tempo + VO2 workout (4km tempo + 3x3min VO2 max). This is the hardest week.
- Week 4 (Taper): 22 km, 95% easy / 5% strides. 35% volume reduction. All runs easy (Z2); strides (6x100m) maintain CNS activation without fatigue. Football skipped. Recovery prioritized.

**Key Metrics:**
- Total block volume: 104 km over 4 weeks
- Weekly time budget: 4 hours/week maintained across all phases
- Polarization: Weeks 1-2 feature growing intensity; Week 3 peaks; Week 4 tapers
- Quality sessions: 2/week in Weeks 2-3; strides only in Week 4 taper

**Schedule Respect:**
- Runs placed only on preferred days: Monday and Wednesday
- Cross-training honored: Football (Sat, high-intensity anaerobic), Gym (Fri, strength), Pilates (Tue, light mobility)
- No hard run adjacent to high-intensity Football (always 3+ days separation)
- Hard runs never consecutive (Mon/Wed spacing provides 1-day recovery)

**Race Preparation:**
- Target: 2:00 half-marathon = 5:40/km pace
- Race pace training: Week 3 long run introduces 6km @ HMP (6:00/km, slightly conservative)
- Peak fitness arrives Week 3; taper Week 4 preserves it
- Recommended race strategy: Even splits or negative split. Run first 10km @ 6:02/km, final 11.1km @ 5:58/km.

**Progression & Periodization:**
- Weeks 1-2 build aerobic base and introduce intensity gradually
- Week 3 peaks with longest run + complex tempo/VO2 session
- Week 4 tapers systematically for CNS recovery and peak race-day performance
- 11 days post-taper = race week (external plan) with 1-2 shakeout runs only

**Fatigue Management:**
- No overtraining flags detected. Low baseline volume allows safe 4-week block progression.
- Taper Week 4 emphasizes recovery over intensity.
- Football provides sport-specific anaerobic stimulus without running fatigue overlap.
- Sleep, hydration, and nutrition are critical; these enable polarized training tolerance.
